Section 1

About Popislicious

💛About Us

Popislicious is a food-based business run by Group 9, Section 5 APK from Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). What started as a class assignment under the Entrepreneurial Acculturation subject grew into a real, hands-on small business experience — our team of 8 handled everything from recipe development and production to marketing, sales, and financial management. We're a group of students who believe a great snack doesn't need to be complicated, just made with care, sold with heart, and shared with a smile.


Our practicum runs on a simple take-order-and-deliver model: customers place their orders with us in advance, and we prepare everything fresh before delivering straight to them (alongside walk-in sales at our pop-up booth).

🌟Vision & Mission

Our Vision

To be the most-loved campus snack brand at UMS — a go-to comfort treat that brings joy in every golden, crunchy bite, and inspires young entrepreneurs to turn simple ideas into real businesses.

Our Mission
  • 🍌  Serve fresh, high-quality Popiah Pisang at affordable prices
  • 🤝  Foster teamwork and hands-on entrepreneurial experience
  • 📣  Build a strong, recognisable brand through creative promotion
  • 💛  Create a warm, customer-first culture in every interaction

🏷️Our Sector

Popislicious operates in the Food & Beverage (F&B) industry, specifically within the snack / street food segment. We focus on affordable, freshly-made treats sold directly to students and staff around the UMS campus through preorders, delivery, and pop-up booths.


Food & Beverage Snack / Street Food Campus Business

🍌What is "Popis"?

"Popis" is short for Popiah Pisang — banana spring rolls. It's our signature snack: ripe banana wrapped in a thin spring roll skin and deep-fried until golden and crunchy on the outside, soft and sweet on the inside. Customers can enjoy it Original (no toppings) or topped with Milky Choco, Matcha Glaze, or Sweet Cheese for extra indulgence.


  • 5 pieces — RM5 (Original, No Toppings)
  • 10 pieces — RM9
  • Toppings +RM1 — Milky Choco / Matcha Glaze / Sweet Cheese
  • Mix Toppings +RM1.50

We also offer delivery (COD) and a pop-up booth at FPPS.

📣How We Promote

We promote Popislicious mainly through our social media platforms, posting teaser, hard-sell, and soft-sell content to build excitement and drive preorders. We also run a pop-up booth at FPPS for walk-in sales, and rely on word-of-mouth from happy customers to grow our reach. (See the Promotion section below for the full breakdown.)

Section 3

How We Promoted

Instagram

Posted teasers, hard-sell and soft-sell content on @popislicious through consistent posting and engaging visuals.

WhatsApp

Used WhatsApp status updates and group chats to reach classmates and friends. Accepting preorders via WhatsApp DM.

Facebook

Shared posts on Facebook to reach a wider campus audience including lecturers and older students.

TikTok

Shared short-form videos on TikTok to showcase our popia-making process, booth highlights, and fun behind-the-scenes moments to reach a wider audience.

Pop-Up Booth

Set up a physical booth at Gazebo FPPS from 12pm–3pm, attracting walk-in customers and live sales.

Digital Posters

Created eye-catching digital promotional posters with our brand aesthetic to announce new flavours, preorders, and booth dates.

Word of Mouth

Happy customers referred us to their friends, which became one of our most effective organic growth channels.

📣 Content Strategy: We used a mix of teaser posts (build anticipation), hard-sell posts (direct product promotion with price), and soft-sell posts (lifestyle, behind-the-scenes, customer features) to keep our audience engaged without feeling overwhelmed by promotions.

Goals & Objectives

  • ✅ Successfully set up a pop-up booth at Gazebo FPPS
  • ✅ Reached 300+ followers on all social media
  • ✅ Launched preorder system and delivery service

Customer Feedback

Swipe through real feedback from our customers:

⭐ "Super crunchy and affordable!"

📱

Social Media Engagement

Our @popislicious social media accounts gained strong traction through consistent posting. With a target of 300 followers per platform set by our lecturer, we are proud to say that we not only met but exceeded that goal across all platforms. Our most successful platform was Facebook, where we achieved an outstanding 968 followers — well beyond the required benchmark. Our content mix of teaser, soft-sell, and hard-sell posts helped maintain high engagement rates throughout the practicum period.

Section 6

Challenges & Suggestions

❗ Problems We Faced

  • Some of our bananas went bad before we could open our weekly booth, resulting in wasted stock and last-minute scrambling.
  • We had no proper kitchen or cooking space of our own to prepare our popia.

✅ Our Solutions

  • We adjusted our purchasing schedule to buy bananas only a day before or on the same day as our booth, keeping our ingredients fresh and reducing waste.
  • We approached the Dean of our faculty to ask for permission, and were able to open and prepare our booth at FPPS.

Suggestions & Recommendations

What we'd do better next time 🤔

01

Assign one member as "order coordinator" during peak hours, dedicated solely to taking and handing out orders. When we were flooded with constant orders, it became hard to track which had already been served and which hadn't — having one point of contact for order flow would prevent mix-ups and missed orders.

02

Introduce a loyalty card where customers can collect stamps for every purchase, and after a certain number of stamps, they receive a free item. This would encourage repeat business and reward our loyal customers.

03

Buy perishable ingredients based on confirmed preorder numbers rather than estimates, so stock like bananas is purchased closer to the actual quantity needed. This builds on what we learned from our spoilage issue and would further minimize waste.
